statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
You are given a set of $ n $ quadratic functions $ F=\{f_1,f_2,\ldots,f_n \} $ , where $ f_i(x)=a_i x^2 + b_i x + c_i $ .

Two functions $ f $ and $ g $ are called independent if $ f(x) \neq g(x) $ for all $ x \in \mathbb{R} $ .

Also, a set of functions $ G=\{g_1,g_2,\ldots,g_k\} $ is called organized if the two functions $ g_i $ and $ g_j $ are independent for all $ 1 \le i \lt j \le |G| $ .

For each $ i=1,2,\ldots,n $ , please find the size of the largest organized subset of $ F $ that contains $ f_i $ as an element.

Input Format
Each test contains multiple test cases. The first line contains the number of test cases $ t $ ( $ 1 \le t \le 10^4 $ ). The description of the test cases follows.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer $ n $ ( $ 1 \le n \le 3000 $ ).

Each of the $ n $ following lines contains three integers $ a_i $ , $ b_i $ , $ c_i $ denoting the function $ f_i $ ( $ -10^6 \le a_i, b_i, c_i \le 10^6 $ , $ a_i \neq 0 $ ).

It is guaranteed that the functions in one test case are pairwise distinct.

It is guaranteed that the sum of $ n^2 $ over all test cases does not exceed $ 3000^2 $ .

Output Format
For each test case, output $ n $ integers $ s_1,s_2,\ldots,s_n $ , where $ s_i $ is the size of the largest organized subset that contains $ f_i $ .

Examples
Sample 1
Input
3
4
1 2 -1
-3 0 -3
-1 4 -5
1 2 -4
5
3 0 0
1 0 -5
-3 0 0
-1 0 10
1 0 -10
5
884 -667 497
680 -973 213
23 -548 -412
826 359 -333
773 212 218
Output
3 2 3 3
3 3 2 2 3
3 3 3 1 2

Note
In the first test case, the functions are as follows:

- $ f_1(x)=x^2+2x-1 $ ;
- $ f_2(x)=-3x^2-3 $ ;
- $ f_3(x)=-x^2+4x-5 $ ;
- $ f_4(x)=x^2+2x-4 $ .

The functions' graphs are as shown below:

![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2195F/b4f354decba5ae994820b330870dab53cf45e34443138cdd21180d1d4fa25035.png)The largest organized subsets of $ F $ containing each function are as follows:

- $ \{f_1,f_3,f_4\} $ is the largest organized subset that contains $ f_1 $ ;
- $ \{f_1,f_2\} $ is the largest organized subset that contains $ f_2 $ ;
- $ \{f_1,f_3,f_4\} $ is the largest organized subset that contains $ f_3 $ ;
- $ \{f_1,f_3,f_4\} $ is the largest organized subset that contains $ f_4 $ .

solutions.title: Editorial
solutions.content:
2195F - Parabola Independence
Let's consider a graph $$$G$$$ where $$$u\leftrightarrow v$$$ is an edge if and only if $$$f_u$$$ and $$$f_v$$$ are independent. Then, by the problem definition, an organized subset is a clique (complete subgraph) in the graph $$$G$$$. The issue is that the clique problem is famously known to be NP-complete. Can we use any other property to our advantage?
The given functions are
continuous
functions. That is, if $$$f_i(x) \neq f_j(x)$$$ for all $$$x \in \mathbb{R}$$$, then either of the following holds:
$$$f_i(x)  \gt  f_j(x)$$$ for all $$$x \in \mathbb{R}$$$.
$$$f_i(x)  \lt  f_j(x)$$$ for all $$$x \in \mathbb{R}$$$.
Let's denote that $$$u \prec v$$$ if $$$f_u(x)  \lt  f_v(x)$$$ for all $$$x \in \mathbb{R}$$$. We will consider the unique properties of this relation.
The relation $$$\prec$$$ is transitive. That is, if $$$a \prec b$$$ and $$$b \prec c$$$, then $$$a \prec c$$$.
If $$$u \prec v$$$, then trivially $$$v \prec u$$$ cannot be true.
$$$u \prec u$$$ cannot hold for any $$$u$$$.
Such a relation is also called a
strict partial order
in mathematics.
Let's construct a directed graph $$$D$$$ where $$$u \to v$$$ is an edge if and only if $$$u \prec v$$$. All indices from an organized subset can be ordered by $$$\prec$$$, so organized subsets of size $$$k$$$ correspond to paths of $$$k$$$ vertices in $$$D$$$. Therefore, now we reduced the problem to a longest path problem in $$$D$$$.
Due to the properties of $$$\prec$$$, the graph $$$D$$$ cannot have cycles, meaning the graph $$$D$$$ is a directed acyclic graph (DAG). As we already know, the longest path problem on a DAG can be solved using DP.
Let's define two DP states. $$$\mathtt{dp}_l[v]$$$ is defined as the length (the number of vertices) of the longest path ending on $$$v$$$, and $$$\mathtt{dp}_r[v]$$$ is defined as the length of the longest path starting on $$$v$$$. Then the length of the longest path containing vertex $$$v$$$ is exactly $$$\mathtt{dp}_l[v]+\mathtt{dp}_r[v]-1$$$. We can compute both $$$\mathtt{dp}_r[v]$$$ and $$$\mathtt{dp}_r[v]$$$ in $$$\mathcal{O}(n^2)$$$ time, and the problem is solved.
